articles.title: 10 Game-Changing Anime Series That Totally Rewrite Genre Rules
articles.content:
From the epic battles of Dragon Ball Z to the psychological depths of Neon Genesis Evangelion, these anime series have pushed the boundaries of their respective genres. They've redefined what it means to be a hero, a villain, and a story.

One of the most influential anime series of all time, Akira (1988) revolutionized the mecha genre with its dark and gritty take on a post-apocalyptic world.

Other series on this list include Fullmetal Alchemist: Brotherhood, which redefined the fantasy genre with its blend of science and magic; Attack on Titan, which turned the typical shonen genre on its head with its dark and intense storyline; Death Note, which redefined the psychological thriller genre with its cat-and-mouse game between a genius detective and a supernatural killer; Haikyuu!!, which redefined the sports genre with its lighthearted and humorous take on high school volleyball; Tokyo Ghoul, which redefined the supernatural genre with its exploration of what it means to be human; Parasyte, which redefined the sci-fi genre with its unique blend of body horror and psychological thriller elements; Gankutsuou: The Count of Monte Cristo, which redefined the historical drama genre with its unique blend of science fiction and classical literature; Ergo Proxy, which redefined the dystopian genre with its exploration of a world where humans and robots coexist; and Paranoia Agent, which redefined the psychological thriller genre with its surreal and dreamlike take on a world where a serial killer is terrorizing Tokyo.

So, what's the next big thing in anime? Will it be a series that pushes the boundaries of our understanding of the world, or one that challenges our assumptions about what it means to be human? Only time will tell.
articles.excerpt: Iconic anime series that have redefined their respective genres and left a lasting impact on the world of animation.
articles.seo_description: Discover the anime series that have redefined their genres and left a lasting impact on the world of animation.

articles.title: Project Hail Mary Full Of Grace: $80M U.S. Opening Breaks Records For Amazon MGM Studios, Lord & Miller And March Non-Franchise Pic – Update
articles.content: SATURDAY PM: Amazon MGM Studios’ Project Hail Mary had a blessed Saturday chalking up $27.1M, just -18% off Friday/preview’s $33.1M which rockets this Ryan Gosling starring, Lord & Miller directed production to a magnificent $80.6M. While that’s the second-best domestic start for a non-franchise movie after Oppenheimer ($82.4M), it’s the best opening for a non-franchise movie in March, surpassing 2022’s The Lost City ($70.3M) and 2011’s Limitless ($18.2M). The sci-fi film has broken multiple records, including the best opening for a non-franchise March movie, according to Box Office Mojo.
articles.excerpt: Amazon MGM Studios' Project Hail Mary breaks records with an $80.6M U.S. opening, surpassing Oppenheimer's second-best domestic start.
articles.seo_description: Amazon MGM Studios' Project Hail Mary breaks records with an $80.6M U.S. opening, surpassing Oppenheimer's second-best domestic start for a non-franchise movie.

articles.title: Guthrie Family Pleads For 'Renewed Attention' To Disappearance As 'No Detail Is Too Small'
articles.content:
A new special on NBC-affiliated KVOA News 4 Tucson, titled Bring Her Home — The Disappearance of Nancy Guthrie, aired this evening, with the Guthrie family providing a statement urging community members to provide any information, no matter how infinitesimal it may seem.

The Sunday editions of Today and NBC Nightly News are to air the special, giving the family's plea for help renewed exposure.

We cannot grieve; we can only ache and wonder,' the family said in a statement, emphasizing that every detail, no matter how small, is crucial in the search for Nancy.
articles.excerpt: The Guthrie family is urging community members to share any information about Nancy's disappearance, no matter how small it may seem.
articles.seo_description: The Guthrie family is searching for any information about Nancy's disappearance, urging community members to share even the smallest details.

articles.title: Amanda Peet Opens Up About Breast Cancer Diagnosis
articles.content: In a heartfelt essay for The New Yorker titled “My Season of Ativan,” actress Amanda Peet revealed her breast cancer diagnosis, which came as both of her parents were undergoing hospice care on opposite coasts. The Something’s Gotta Give star had been seeing a breast surgeon biannually for checkups, and prior to Labor Day last year. Peet is undergoing treatment, but it is unclear what stage her cancer is at.
articles.excerpt: Actress Amanda Peet reveals breast cancer diagnosis in a heartfelt essay for The New Yorker.
articles.seo_description: Actress Amanda Peet opens up about her breast cancer diagnosis in a heartfelt essay for The New Yorker.

articles.title: Saturday Night Live UK Is A Laughter-Free Yawn That’s Not A Patch On Lorne Michaels’ American Original
articles.content: Saturday Night Live UK. What is it?! Painful, that’s what. Yeah, seriously. Beyond seriously unfunny. The show launched on Sky Saturday night with a dire Cold Open featuring prime minister Keir Starmer quivering over taking a call from Donald Trump where not one single memorable line was uttered. Tina Fey popped up as guest host.
articles.excerpt: The UK version of Saturday Night Live fails to deliver laughs and falls short of its American counterpart.
articles.seo_description: Saturday Night Live UK fails to impress in its latest episode, lacking the humor and charm of its American counterpart.

articles.title: Vin Diesel Hints at Emotional Conclusion to 'Fast & Furious' Franchise
articles.content: Vin Diesel Pens Tribute To ‘Fast & Furious’ Franchise, Teases Final Installment: “There Is A Particular Weight That Comes With Delivering A Finale”  In a lengthy social media post paying tribute to the legacy of the Fast and Furious franchise, star Vin Diesel noted the “responsibility” he feels to deliver on a satisfying finale. The action-crime saga, which began in 2001 with the first installment, will wrap up in March 2028.  “25 years. Eight directors. Countless writers, crew members, stuntmen and stuntwomen, and the countless families they support,” Diesel wrote. “The weight of this responsibility is crushing at times, but the passion and love that drives us to make this movie makes it all worthwhile.”  Diesel also expressed his gratitude to the franchise's fans, saying, “To the fans, thank you for 25 years of love and support. It's not just about the movies – it's about the community, the family, the friendship.”  The final installment of the franchise is set to release on March 17, 2028, and Diesel hinted that it will be an emotional conclusion to the saga. “There is a particular weight that comes with delivering a finale,” he said. “But we're going to do it justice, and we're going to make it one for the ages.”  The Fast and Furious franchise has become a cultural phenomenon over the years, grossing over $5 billion at the box office and cementing its place as one of the most successful film franchises of all time. With the final installment on the horizon, fans are eagerly awaiting what's in store for Dominic Toretto and his crew.
articles.excerpt: Vin Diesel hints at emotional conclusion to Fast and Furious franchise, teases final installment.
articles.seo_description: Vin Diesel hints at emotional conclusion to Fast and Furious franchise, teases final installment.
